other_observers: none; Steve Larson (original finder) I received an email from Nick Komar stating that his son and Cole Wild saw the bird.
specific_location: The bird was seen at Banner Lakes SWA, Weld County, on the West side of pond 11
description: Size and shape: About 2 ft. tall, similar in shape to the Black-crowneds but slimmer and longer necked. Bill: Stout, pointed and all dark in color.  Head: Face and crown heavily streaked grayish brown, darker on the crown. Eye: Reddish orange. Upper parts: Neck, back and wings grayish brown with fine white tips on the feathers. Under parts: Neck and breast heavily streaked grayish brown becoming paler on the belly. Under wings grayish brown. Legs: Long and yellowishBehaviors: Behavior: The birds were observed standing at the edge of the marsh and as I approached for a better look eventually flew to the East side of the pond.Call: NAPlumage: Immature/juvinile
